channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Beginner’s Roadmap to Learning Artificial Intelligence on AWS
item.description: Artificial Intelligence today isn’t just a frontier—it’s infrastructure. And if you’re stepping into this space, Amazon Web Services (AWS) offers one of the most practical, scalable environments...

item.title: Self-Hosting in 2026: The Complete Infrastructure Stack (82 Containers, $0 Cloud Bill)
item.description: Originally published on TechSaaS Cloud Originally published on TechSaaS Cloud Self-Hosting in 2026: The Complete Infrastructure Stack We run 82 production containers on a single physical server....

item.title: Rethinking Data Engineering: Why ETL Pipelines Still Take Too Long — and a New Way Forward
item.description: Over the past decade, platforms like Databricks and Snowflake have significantly improved how organizations handle data storage, scalability, and distributed processing. However, one critical...

item.title: Why I built a local terminal workspace instead of using tmux
item.description: I wanted a terminal workspace that behaves more like a normal desktop app. Something that: keeps tabs and splits restores layout and working directories after restart uses normal Ctrl+C / Ctrl+V...

item.title: How I Built an MCP Server for Media & Entertainment Intelligence
item.description: Give your AI agent real-time knowledge about Netflix, Disney, and 30+ media companies. If you work in media and entertainment — or invest in it — you know the information landscape is brutal....

item.title: Subqueries vs CTEs in SQL: A Practical Guide for Data Analysts
item.description: 💡 How I Finally Understood Subqueries vs CTEs When I first started learning SQL, I remember feeling confident—until I ran into subqueries. At first, they seemed simple. “Just a query inside...

item.title: Cache-Aside and the Null Sentinel Pattern
item.description: There is a class of bugs that are worse than crashes. Crashes are loud, they page someone and they get fixed. This one is quiet. Your cache is running, your hit rate looks fine. But your database...

item.title: Stop Manually Fixing Your Agent’s Output: How We Built a Custom Skill for Monday.com
item.description: I'm using my AI agent to create Monday.com tasks. I ask Claude Code to create a task with a proper description, and it does - except when I open the task, the description field is empty. The text...

item.title: The AI writing tic I couldn't stop seeing after building a humanizer
item.description: The AI writing tic I couldn't stop seeing after building a humanizer I built unslop to strip the tells that mark text as AI-generated. I thought I knew what they were. I was wrong about which ones...

item.title: How AI-900 Helps Beginners Understand Machine Learning and AI
item.description: The journey into Artificial Intelligence often feels like stepping into a room where everyone already speaks a language you don’t yet understand. The certification Microsoft Azure AI Fundamentals...

item.title: Meta will record employees’ keystrokes and use it to train its AI models
item.description: Meta says that it has a new internal tool that is converting mouse movements and button clicks into data that can train its AI models.

item.title: Unauthorized group has gained access to Anthropic’s exclusive cyber tool Mythos, report claims
item.description: Anthropic told TechCrunch it is investigating the claims, but maintains that there is no evidence that its systems have been impacted.

item.title: SpaceX is working with Cursor and has an option to buy the startup for $60B
item.description: The move could shore up weaknesses at each company, but it also reveals them. Neither Cursor nor xAI has proprietary models that can match the leading offerings from Anthropic and OpenAI — the...

item.title: SpaceX cuts a deal to maybe buy Cursor for $60 billion
item.description: With an IPO looming for Elon Musk's SpaceX / xAI / X combo platter of companies, SpaceX has announced an odd arrangement to either acquire the automated programming platform Cursor for $60 billion...

item.title: Roundtables: Unveiling The 10 Things That Matter in AI Right Now
item.description: Listen to the session or watch below Watch a special edition of Roundtables simulcast live from EmTech AI, MIT Technology Review’s signature conference for AI leadership. Subscribers got an...

item.title: We translated the Palantir manifesto for actual human beings
item.description: Palantir CEO Alex Karp is a man in charge of one of the most important and frightening companies in the world. Karp's new book, cowritten with Nicholas Zamiska, is called The Technological...

item.title: The new word in home construction could be “plastics”
item.description: Single-use plastics are a persistent source of environmental pollution, and the need to house a growing global population puts increasing pressure on resources such as timber. MIT engineers have...

item.title: A natural protein may protect the GI tract from infection
item.description: Embedded in the body’s mucosal surfaces, proteins called lectins bind to sugars found on cell surfaces. A team led by MIT chemistry professor Laura Kiessling has found that one such protein,...

item.title: This tool could show how consciousness works
item.description: How does the physical matter in our brains translate into thoughts, sensations, and emotions? It’s hard to explore that question without neurosurgery. But in a recent paper, MIT philosopher...

item.title: Early life may have breathed oxygen earlier than believed
item.description: Around 2.3 billion years ago, a pivotal period known as the Great Oxidation Event set the evolutionary course for oxygen-breathing life on Earth. But MIT geobiologists and colleagues have found...

item.title: Analog computing from waste heat
item.description: Heat generated by electronic devices is usually a problem, but a team led by Giuseppe Romano, a research scientist at MIT’s Institute for Soldier Nanotechnologies, has found a way to use it for...

item.title: Get ready for hotter, muggier, stormier summers
item.description: A long stretch of humid heat followed by a powerful thunderstorm is a familiar weather pattern in the tropics, but it’s also becoming more common in midlatitude regions such as the US Midwest. A...

item.title: ISS astronauts are getting new laptops
item.description: Even astronauts need to level up their laptops once in a while - including the crew of Expedition 74 on board the ISS, which NASA announced last week is in the process of some computer upgrades....

item.title: Tim Cook was an innovator — just not the Jobs kind
item.description: Under Steve Jobs, Apple released the groundbreaking products that defined the company. But the company wouldn't be what it is today without Tim Cook's reign of ruthless efficiency. Jobs' legacy...

item.title: AI backlash is coming for elections
item.description: Ask Americans how they feel about AI and most say they have concerns. Communities have mounted resistance to data center projects, stalling them across the US. On social media, anger at AI...

item.title: OpenAI’s updated image generator can now pull information from the web
item.description: OpenAI is rolling out the latest version of its AI-powered image generator with new "thinking capabilities," allowing it to search the web to help it create multiple images from a single prompt....

item.title: Framework’s Laptop 13 Pro launch event
item.description: Framework CEO Nirav Patel is showing off his company’s latest modular, repairable laptops in San Francisco today. The headliner is the new Laptop 13 Pro, which is its first machine fully machined...

item.title: X makes it 1,900 percent more expensive to post links
item.description: Posting links to X through custom social media software just got a lot more expensive. On Monday, X significantly increased how much it costs to post a URL via the X API, which is what third-party...

item.title: SystemBC C2 Server Reveals 1,570+ Victims in The Gentlemen Ransomware Operation
item.description: Threat actors associated with The Gentlemen ransomware‑as‑a‑service (RaaS) operation have been observed attempting to deploy a known proxy malware called SystemBC. According to new research...

item.title: Framework announces Laptop 13 Pro, ‘the MacBook Pro for Linux users’
item.description: Every time we review a Framework laptop, we find familiar pros and cons. They're truly upgradable, incredibly repairable, but we always wish the battery lasted longer. We always wish the build...
